## Summary

Fixes the login race condition where users had to click "Sign in" multiple times, and the sign-in took forever to complete. Also migrates the signup page from old gray/indigo styling to the rg-ink editorial design language, and fixes OAuth callback error handling to show visible errors instead of silent redirect loops.

## Scope

- `app/login/page.tsx` — session sync polling, callback error display, consolidated router refs
- `app/signup/page.tsx` — session sync polling, full rg-ink brand restyle
- `app/api/auth/callback/route.ts` — error redirect on failed code exchange

Out of scope: evaluation pipeline, API workers, prompts, middleware logic unchanged.

## Visual Evidence

| State | Before | After |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Login | Multiple clicks required; no feedback during cookie sync | Single click, polls server session up to 3s before navigating |
| Signup | Gray/indigo generic Tailwind UI | rg-ink/rg-cream/rg-gold editorial brand (matches login/forgot/reset) |
| OAuth failure | Silent redirect loop (callback → dashboard → login → repeat) | Shows "Sign-in failed. Please try again." on login page |

## Accessibility

- All form inputs retain existing ARIA labels and focus order from login page pattern
- Signup page now uses same high-contrast rg-cream on rg-ink as login (improved from low-contrast gray-on-white)
- Error messages use rg-red/60 border with rg-cream2 text for visibility

## Browser Targets

- Chrome latest: tested
- Safari latest: expected compatible (same CSS patterns as login page)
- Firefox latest: expected compatible

## Unauthorized Input Sources

- `?error=callback_failed` query parameter on /login — read-only, checked against exact string literal, no injection risk
- `/api/auth/user` polled during session sync — existing authenticated endpoint, no new input surface

## Internal Process Leakage

- No internal IDs, worker state, or stack traces exposed
- OAuth callback error redirects to generic "Sign-in failed" message — no Supabase error details leaked to client

## Input → Action → Output

**Login flow:**
1. User submits email/password → `signInWithPassword` call
2. On success → poll `/api/auth/user` up to 10x (300ms apart)
3. Once server confirms session → `router.push('/dashboard')`
4. If polling times out after ~3s → navigate anyway (graceful degradation)

**OAuth callback failure:**
1. `/api/auth/callback?code=xxx` → `exchangeCodeForSession` fails
2. Redirect to `/login?error=callback_failed`
3. Login page reads `?error` param → shows "Sign-in failed. Please try again."

## Public-Safe Quality/Status Metrics

N/A — no new metrics or status indicators added. Existing telemetry events preserved.

## Runtime/Pipeline Expansion

None — no new API routes, workers, or pipeline paths. The `/api/auth/user` endpoint is existing and already called on page load.

## Latency Impact

- Login: adds up to ~3s worst-case polling (10 attempts × 300ms). Typically the session syncs in <1s (1-3 polls). This replaces the previous behavior where users clicked Sign In multiple times over 10+ seconds.
- Signup: identical polling pattern, only activates on instant-session signup flow.
- Net improvement: single-click login instead of multi-click retry.

## Branch Freshness (Never Behind)

Branch-Behind-Base: 0

## Risks & Anomalies

- Session polling adds a brief delay (~300-900ms typically) before navigation. This is intentional — the alternative was multiple failed navigations and a confusing login experience.
- If `/api/auth/user` is slow or down, the polling times out after ~3s and navigates anyway — same behavior as before, just with a timeout instead of immediate race.

---

No-Pipeline-Impact: Confirmed — this PR does not modify lib/evaluation/**, app/api/workers/**, prompts, or any pipeline contract.
